speaker: PERICLES
text:
Let none disturb us.
                 
                (
                Attendant
                 
                exits
                .
                )
               Why should this change of thoughts,

speaker: PERICLES
text: The sad companion dull-eyed Melancholy,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Be my so used a guest as not an hour

speaker: PERICLES
text: In the day’s glorious walk or peaceful night,

speaker: PERICLES
text: The tomb where grief should sleep, can breed me quiet?

speaker: PERICLES
text: Here pleasures court mine eyes, and mine eyes shun them;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And danger, which I feared, is at Antioch,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Whose arm seems far too short to hit me here.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Yet neither pleasure’s art can joy my spirits,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Nor yet the other’s distance comfort me.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Then it is thus: the passions of the mind

speaker: PERICLES
text: That have their first conception by misdread

speaker: PERICLES
text: Have after-nourishment and life by care;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And what was first but fear what might be done

speaker: PERICLES
text: Grows elder now, and cares it be not done.

speaker: PERICLES
text: And so with me. The great Antiochus,

speaker: PERICLES
text: ’Gainst whom I am too little to contend,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Since he’s so great can make his will his act,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Will think me speaking though I swear to silence;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Nor boots it me to say I honor him

speaker: PERICLES
text: If he suspect I may dishonor him.

speaker: PERICLES
text: And what may make him blush in being known,

speaker: PERICLES
text: He’ll stop the course by which it might be known.

speaker: PERICLES
text: With hostile forces he’ll o’er-spread the land,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And with th’ ostent of war will look so huge

speaker: PERICLES
text: Amazement shall drive courage from the state,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Our men be vanquished ere they do resist,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And subjects punished that ne’er thought offense;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which care of them, not pity of myself,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who am no more but as the tops of trees

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which fence the roots they grow by and defend them,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Makes both my body pine and soul to languish

speaker: PERICLES
text: And punish that before that he would punish.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Joy and all comfort in your sacred breast.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: And keep your mind till you return to us

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Peaceful and comfortable.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Peace, peace, and give experience tongue.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: They do abuse the King that flatter him,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: For flattery is the bellows blows up sin;

speaker: HELICANUS
text: The thing the which is flattered, but a spark

speaker: HELICANUS
text: To which that wind gives heat and stronger glowing;

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Whereas reproof, obedient and in order,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Fits kings as they are men, for they may err.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: When Signior Sooth here does proclaim peace,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: He flatters you, makes war upon your life.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Prince, pardon me, or strike me, if you please.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: I cannot be much lower than my knees.

speaker: PERICLES
text: All leave us else; but let your cares o’erlook

speaker: PERICLES
text: What shipping and what lading’s in our haven,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And then return to us.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Helicanus,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou hast moved us. What seest thou in our looks?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: An angry brow, dread lord.

speaker: PERICLES
text: If there be such a dart in princes’ frowns,

speaker: PERICLES
text: How durst thy tongue move anger to our face?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: How dares the plants look up to heaven,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: From whence they have their nourishment?

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou knowest I have power to take thy life from thee.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: I have ground the ax myself;

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Do but you strike the blow.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Rise, prithee rise.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Sit down. Thou art no flatterer.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I thank thee for ’t; and heaven forbid

speaker: PERICLES
text: That kings should let their ears hear their faults hid.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Fit counselor and servant for a prince,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who by thy wisdom makes a prince thy servant,

speaker: PERICLES
text: What wouldst thou have me do?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: To bear with patience such griefs

speaker: HELICANUS
text: As you yourself do lay upon yourself.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou speak’st like a physician, Helicanus,

speaker: PERICLES
text: That ministers a potion unto me

speaker: PERICLES
text: That thou wouldst tremble to receive thyself.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Attend me, then: I went to Antioch,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Where, as thou know’st, against the face of death

speaker: PERICLES
text: I sought the purchase of a glorious beauty

speaker: PERICLES
text: From whence an issue I might propagate,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Are arms to princes and bring joys to subjects.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Her face was to mine eye beyond all wonder,

speaker: PERICLES
text: The rest—hark in thine ear—as black as incest,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which by my knowledge found, the sinful father

speaker: PERICLES
text: Seemed not to strike, but smooth. But thou know’st this:

speaker: PERICLES
text: ’Tis time to fear when tyrants seems to kiss;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which fear so grew in me I hither fled

speaker: PERICLES
text: Under the covering of a careful night,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who seemed my good protector; and, being here,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Bethought me what was past, what might succeed.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I knew him tyrannous, and tyrants’ fears

speaker: PERICLES
text: Decrease not but grow faster than the years;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And should he doubt, as no doubt he doth,

speaker: PERICLES
text: That I should open to the list’ning air

speaker: PERICLES
text: How many worthy princes’ bloods were shed

speaker: PERICLES
text: To keep his bed of blackness unlaid ope,

speaker: PERICLES
text: To lop that doubt he’ll fill this land with arms,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And make pretense of wrong that I have done him;

speaker: PERICLES
text: When all, for mine—if I may call ’t—offense,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Must feel war’s blow, who spares not innocence;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which love to all—of which thyself art one,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who now reproved’st me for ’t—

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Alas, sir!

speaker: PERICLES
text: Drew sleep out of mine eyes, blood from my cheeks,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Musings into my mind, with thousand doubts

speaker: PERICLES
text: How I might stop this tempest ere it came;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And finding little comfort to relieve them,

speaker: PERICLES
text: I thought it princely charity to grieve for them.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Well, my lord, since you have given me leave to speak,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Freely will I speak. Antiochus you fear,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: And justly too, I think, you fear the tyrant,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Who either by public war or private treason

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Will take away your life.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Therefore, my lord, go travel for a while,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Till that his rage and anger be forgot,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Or till the Destinies do cut his thread of life.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Your rule direct to any. If to me,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Day serves not light more faithful than I’ll be.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I do not doubt thy faith.

speaker: PERICLES
text: But should he wrong my liberties in my absence?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: We’ll mingle our bloods together in the earth,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: From whence we had our being and our birth.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Tyre, I now look from thee, then, and to Tarsus

speaker: PERICLES
text: Intend my travel, where I’ll hear from thee,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And by whose letters I’ll dispose myself.

speaker: PERICLES
text: The care I had and have of subjects’ good

speaker: PERICLES
text: On thee I lay, whose wisdom’s strength can bear it.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I’ll take thy word for faith, not ask thine oath.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who shuns not to break one will crack both.

speaker: PERICLES
text: But in our orbs we’ll live so round and safe

speaker: PERICLES
text: That time of both this truth shall ne’er convince.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou showed’st a subject’s shine, I a true prince.
